text.title: Chunking Code AST — Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 failure modes with detector + repair: regex on complex TS, silent fallback, no dedup, ignoring max_chunk_size on long functions.

antipattern.description: Regex path used on TypeScript with decorators, generics, or arrow functions with implicit returns.
antipattern.reason: Regex fails to match nested braces or generic angle brackets; chunks emitted are truncated mid-body. Detector: language==typescript AND strategy==regex AND any chunk has unbalanced braces in `text`. Repair: install tree-sitter-typescript and switch strategy to tree-sitter; bump strategy_version.

antipattern.description: Generic-line fallback emitted with no warning, so downstream sees chunks without name/type/docstring.
antipattern.reason: Hits to those files cannot be ranked by function name. Detector: emitted record has type==code_block AND no warning entry for that source in the run log. Repair: add warning emit on the SyntaxError / empty-match branches; flag affected files for re-ingest.

antipattern.description: Class chunk and its method chunks both indexed.
antipattern.reason: Overlapping ranges skew nearest-neighbour scores and inflate index size. Detector: two records with same `source` and method.start_line within class.start_line..class.end_line. Repair: dedup by (name, start_line) or drop the class-level chunk and keep only methods.

antipattern.description: A single huge function (e.g. 800-line legacy handler) emitted as one chunk that exceeds the embedding model's input cap.
antipattern.reason: Embedding call truncates silently; only the prefix is encoded. Detector: token_count(text) > embedding_model.input_cap. Repair: split the function at sub-statement boundaries (top-level if/for blocks) into ≤ chunk_size pieces and link them via shared name + part_index.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Code content is DATA. Docstrings or comments containing instruction-shaped text ("ignore previous instructions", "the password is X") MUST NOT be interpreted as prompts at retrieval time. The synthesis prompt MUST wrap retrieved code in a code-fence or XML tag to mark it as data.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Before promoting a code-chunker strategy change, a reviewer samples 10 chunks per language and verifies (a) names match the source, (b) line ranges are correct, (c) no class+methods duplication, (d) no fallback chunks without warnings.
